key_insight: RNC-LM improves Levenberg-Marquardt by using Riemann normal coordinates to add higher-order geometric corrections, making finite optimization steps more consistent with the local nonlinear manifold.
engineering_relevance: It can make nonlinear least-squares training more stable and accurate in curved, ill-conditioned problems such as PINNs, regression, and large-scale surrogate/model fitting.
method: Reformulates the geodesic equation in a moving tangent frame to compute higher-order update corrections.
Uses Riemann-normal-coordinate updates to reduce parameter-effects curvature beyond standard geodesic acceleration.
Adds a line search along the RNC curve to control step length while preserving low cost.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved convergence on classical benchmarks, reduced PINN relative L2 error to around 1e-3, and a 34-fold speedup on a large-scale potential-energy-surface fitting task.
watch_item: Performance may depend on accurate higher-order geometric terms and a well-tuned line search, which could add implementation complexity in large models.
paper_title: Higher-Order Geometric Updates for Levenberg-Marquardt Method via Riemann Normal Coordinates
